resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def removeZeroSumSublists(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head
        prefix_sum = 0
        seen = {0: dummy}
        while head:
            prefix_sum += head.val
            if prefix_sum in seen:
                node = seen[prefix_sum]
                temp = node.next
                temp_prefix_sum = prefix_sum + temp.val
                while temp != head:
                    del seen[temp_prefix_sum]
                    temp = temp.next
                    temp_prefix_sum += temp.val
                node.next = head.next
            else:
                seen[prefix_sum] = head
            head = head.next
        return dummy.next
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a hash map to store the prefix sum of the linked list. It iterates through the linked list, updating the prefix sum and checking if it exists in the hash map. If it does, it removes the corresponding sublist by updating the next pointer of the node in the hash map. This approach has a high efficiency due to its constant time complexity for hash map operations.
